questions.question: A bacteria population doubles every 3 hours. If there are 50 bacteria at the start, how many bacteria will there be after 6 hours?
questions.explanation: After 6 hours, two doubling periods have passed (6 ÷ 3 = 2). The population is 50 × 2² = 50 × 4 = 200.

questions.question: A radioactive substance decays to half its mass every 5 years. If you start with 80 grams, what mass remains after 10 years?
questions.explanation: After 10 years, two half-lives have passed (10 ÷ 5 = 2). The mass is 80 × (1/2)² = 80 × 1/4 = 20 grams.

questions.question: Write the exponential decay formula if a substance has an initial value of 1000 and decays at a rate of 15% per year. Use t for time in years.
questions.explanation: In exponential decay, A = A₀(1 − r)^t. Here A₀ = 1000 and r = 0.15, so A = 1000(0.85)^t.

questions.question: An investment of $500 grows at 10% per year. What is the value after 2 years if compounded annually?
If a population triples every 4 years and starts at 100, how many years will it take to reach 900?
questions.explanation: After t years, population = 100 × 3^(t/4). Set 100 × 3^(t/4) = 900, so 3^(t/4) = 9 = 3². Thus t/4 = 2, giving t = 8 years.

questions.question: A car depreciates at 20% per year. If it costs $12,000 when new, what is its value after 1 year?
